mission.title: Install and Configure Kubeflow
mission.description: Kubeflow is a machine learning toolkit for Kubernetes that provides a comprehensive platform for building, deploying, and managing machine learning workflows. It is particularly useful for teams looking to implement scalable AI solutions using Kubernetes. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for Kubernetes expertise.

steps.title: Install the Kubeflow CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kubeflow CLI, which is essential for managing your Kubeflow deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow/releases/download/v1.10.0/kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv kubeflow /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Kubeflow CLI tarball.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster where Kubeflow will be deployed.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kubeflow-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.30.0

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.
commands: gcloud container clusters create kubeflow-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.30.0

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --zone us-central1-a

```

> Fetch cluster credentials.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --zone us-central1-a

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your cluster are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Deploy Kubeflow
steps.description:
This step deploys Kubeflow to your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -k github.com/kubeflow/manifests/kustomize/overlays/cluster

```

> Deploy Kubeflow using Kustomize.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures additional features like monitoring and autoscaling.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/master/monitoring/monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/master/autoscaling/autoscaling.yaml

```

> Deploy monitoring components.
> Deploy autoscaling components.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kubeflow
uninstall.description:
This step removes Kubeflow from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and data.

```bash

kubectl delete -k github.com/kubeflow/manifests/kustomize/overlays/cluster

gcloud container clusters delete kubeflow-cluster --zone us-central1-a --quiet

```

> Remove Kubeflow deployment.
> Delete the GKE cluster and all orphaned resources.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete kubeflow-cluster --zone us-central1-a --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kubeflow
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your data. Then, perform the upgrade and verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl get all -o yaml > kubeflow-backup.yaml

kubectl delete -k github.com/kubeflow/manifests/kustomize/overlays/cluster

kubectl apply -k github.com/kubeflow/manifests/kustomize/overlays/cluster

kubectl get pods -n kubeflow-system

```

> Backup current resources.
> Remove the current Kubeflow installation.
> Re-deploy the latest version of Kubeflow.
> Verify that all pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl CLI is not installed or not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl and ensure it's in your PATH.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --zone us-central1-a' to set up kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'Pending' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Check node resources and consider resizing the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl apply failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubeflow manifests may be incompatible with your Kubernetes version.

**Fix:**
Ensure you are using a supported Kubernetes version (1.27, 1.28, 1.29, 1.30).

**Affected versions:** <= 1.30

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new ML frameworks, improved integration with Kubernetes 1.30 features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain APIs related to model serving.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.9, ensure to update your CRDs and check for deprecated APIs.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kubeflow-system' showing all pods in 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: gcloud container clusters create kubeflow-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.30.0
gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --zone us-central1-a

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a GCP account and the necessary permissions to create Kubernetes clusters.
